questions.scenario: A folder has one Cargo.toml and a src directory. What article word names the project Cargo manages?
questions.explanation: A package is the Cargo-managed project described by Cargo.toml.

questions.scenario: What are src/main.rs and src/lib.rs in the article vocabulary?
questions.explanation: A crate root is where Rust starts reading a binary or library crate.

questions.scenario: A file named src/parser.rs exists, but lib.rs does not declare mod parser; or pub mod parser;. What does the article say?

options.text: The file is not automatically part of the crate just because it exists.
Cargo will always include it by file name.
It becomes a dependency in Cargo.lock.

questions.explanation: Rust asks the crate root to declare modules explicitly.
